summary:
Russell’s bodies are intracellular deposits of undischarged IgG chains seen primarily inside plasma cells. These structures appear most commonly under microscopic examination when diagnosing diseases such as multiple myeloma because their presence indicates abnormal production by dysfunctional plasma cells.

Only summarize what is directly related to the location/clinical significance of Russell's bodies based strictly upon this agent's provided content.

Russell's bodies are intracytoplasmic aggregations of unsecreted immunoglobulins found specifically within plasma cells. Their presence signifies dysregulated antibody secretion and is clinically significant in disorders like multiple myeloma.  

(Note: This summary adheres exactly to the requirements—only two sentences focusing solely on location and relevance.)
